extraction.one_line_summary: VENDRAMI Andrea won from breakaway after 70km of attacks, beating HAMILTON Chris in two-up sprint while NIBALI descended to gain seconds on GC favorites.
tactical_patterns: Trek's BRAMBILA arguing with BENNETT in breakaway created gap exploitation opportunity for HAMILTON on final climb.
NIBALI attacked on final descent using descending skills to gain time, forcing Ineos to chase rather than teammates.
CICCONE attacked with teammate NIBALI before final climb, requiring domestique-less Ineos response revealing team isolation.

rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: positive: attacked final descent gaining seconds on GC group despite Ineos chase, showing descending remains viable attacking weapon
rider_signals.BRAMBILA Gianluca: negative: argued with BENNETT in breakaway allowing HAMILTON gap on final climb, tactical immaturity cost stage podium
rider_signals.HAMILTON Chris: positive: exploited BRAMBILA-BENNETT dispute to gain gap, nearly won stage despite being outnumbered
rider_signals.MOSCON Gianni: negative: crashed descending while chasing NIBALI attack, compromising Ineos chase capacity
rider_signals.CICCONE Giulio: neutral: attacked with NIBALI before finale but Trek domestique role limited personal opportunity
